reasoning: The market probability was 52% on April 2, 2026, but the evidence heavily favors a pivot away from a native US Dollar stablecoin. In October 2025, Revolut successfully rolled out 1:1 fee-free conversions for existing USD stablecoins (USDC and USDT), which satisfies user demand for a USD stablecoin without Revolut absorbing the regulatory and reserve burdens. Furthermore, recent reports from February 2026 indicate that Revolut is now actively exploring a UK-regulated, Sterling-backed (GBP) stablecoin, aligning with their newly acquired UK banking license in March 2026. With only 8 months left in 2026 and no concrete announcements or active regulatory filings for a native USD stablecoin, a functional and public launch in 2026 is unlikely. The strategic pivot to leveraging existing USD tokens while developing a native GBP token makes sense given the market dynamics (dominance of Tether/USDC in USD, gap in the GBP market). Thus, the probability of a USD stablecoin launch this year has significantly diminished.
